title: Yuma Mesa — South Desert Plateau
qualification_reasons: Top-tier direct normal irradiance with minimal cloud cover
Flat desert plateau with <2% slope
Large contiguous BLM / state-trust parcels available
Proximate to existing 230kV transmission corridor
caution_notes: Desert tortoise habitat overlay requires environmental review
Summer dust storms and soiling affect O&M assumptions
Confirm water rights for panel washing and construction
gemini_summary_seed: The Yuma Mesa site is a textbook utility-scale desert candidate: high irradiance, near-zero slope, cheap land, and an existing high-voltage corridor within reach.

title: Gila Bend Flats — Central Corridor
qualification_reasons: Existing cluster of operating utility-scale solar plants validates siting
Flat alluvial terrain with excellent panel layout economics
Short feeder distance to APS transmission infrastructure
caution_notes: Interconnection queue in Maricopa pocket is actively congesting
gemini_summary_seed: Gila Bend is a proven utility-scale corridor — geography, land prices, and transmission are all favorable, but queue timing now dominates project risk.

title: Kingman Valley — Northwest AZ
qualification_reasons: Strong DNI with cooler panel temperatures improving yield
Wide-open valley with predominantly low-slope parcels
Near Mead–Phoenix transmission path
caution_notes: Parcelization is more fragmented than central AZ
Verify Hualapai Tribe consultation requirements for nearby parcels
gemini_summary_seed: Kingman offers a cooler-climate variant on southwestern utility-scale solar — slightly further from load but with good transmission access.

title: Safford Basin — Eastern AZ
qualification_reasons: Broad, low-slope basin with consistent irradiance
Agricultural land conversion economics are attractive
Accessible SRP/TEP transmission via existing substations
caution_notes: Competition for irrigation-rights parcels with agricultural users
gemini_summary_seed: Safford Basin screens as a solid secondary AZ option: strong solar, cheap flat land, moderate transmission, with agriculture competing for the best parcels.

title: Willcox Playa — Southeast AZ
qualification_reasons: Extremely flat playa-adjacent terrain minimizes earthworks
Strong year-round irradiance with low cloud cover
Historical agricultural land with clear ownership structure
caution_notes: Playa-adjacent hydrology requires stormwater design review
Migratory bird overlay near Willcox Playa IBA
gemini_summary_seed: Willcox offers excellent physical fundamentals for utility-scale PV, with playa hydrology and avian overlays as the primary diligence items.

title: Eldorado Valley — South NV
qualification_reasons: Home to some of the largest operating U.S. solar plants — proven corridor
Adjacent to major 500kV transmission toward Las Vegas load center
Federally designated BLM solar energy zone with streamlined permitting
caution_notes: Queue congestion in southern NV is severe
Desert tortoise critical habitat requires mitigation planning
gemini_summary_seed: Eldorado Valley is arguably the most proven utility-scale solar basin in North America; physical fundamentals are excellent and only queue access constrains new entrants.

title: Pahrump Valley — Southwest NV
qualification_reasons: Excellent DNI with low cloud cover
Broad, low-slope valley with large BLM tracts
Short haul to Las Vegas and southern CA load
caution_notes: Water-rights constraints are significant in Nye County
Endangered species surveys often required pre-construction
gemini_summary_seed: Pahrump is a strong second-tier Nevada basin with solid physical characteristics; environmental diligence and transmission routing dominate project timelines.

title: Deming Plains — Southwest NM
qualification_reasons: High-desert irradiance rivals southern Arizona
Large private and state-trust parcels at attractive prices
Near SunZia / proposed transmission corridors
caution_notes: SunZia and related corridor timelines still have delivery risk
Water permitting for construction is a local friction
gemini_summary_seed: Deming offers AZ-quality solar economics with cheaper land; the bet is whether new transmission lands on schedule.

title: Roswell Basin — East NM
qualification_reasons: Broad flat basin with year-round sun and low humidity
Access to Permian load growth and expanding transmission
Low-cost ranch-land conversion opportunity
caution_notes: High local dust load affects soiling and O&M modeling
Verify co-location with oil & gas lease overlays
gemini_summary_seed: Roswell benefits from Permian demand and cheap flat land; soiling and lease overlays require careful diligence but fundamentals are strong.
